title: Default network gets created when using Docker Compose
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

Regression

**Description**

When creating a container using Docker Compose, a default network gets created. This issue was not observed with earlier versions of podman.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Make sure all (macvlan) network interfaces are created beforehand
```
# podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME        DRIVER
39093c071800  ens160      macvlan
22e496b0a5ff  ens192      macvlan
2f259bab93aa  podman      bridge
#
```

2. Create a Docker Compose file with one of the macvlan network
```
# cat docker-compose.yml
    networks:
      ens160:
        ipv4_address: 10.xx.xxx.xxx
...
networks:
  ens160:
    external:
      name: ens160
...
```

3. Start a container using Docker Compose using the network interfaces
```
# docker-compose up -d --no-deps mycont
Creating network "tmp_default" with the default driver
Creating mycont ...
Creating mycont ... error

ERROR: for mycont  Cannot create container for service mycont_tool: container create: cannot set network aliases for network "tmp_default" because dns is disabled: invalid argument

ERROR: for mycont  Cannot create container for service mycont_tool: container create: cannot set network aliases for network "tmp_default" because dns is disabled: invalid argument
ERROR: Encountered errors while bringing up the project.

# podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME         DRIVER
39093c071800  ens160       macvlan
22e496b0a5ff  ens192       macvlan
2f259bab93aa  podman       bridge
89934527af27  tmp_default  bridge
#
```

**Describe the results you received:**
A new unexpected tmp_default network is auto created that fails for some DNS reasons

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container should get started without creating any new network interface

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Always

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.17.5
Git Commit:   d847ad598d752dad479250b7f289858cc272cb04
Built:        Fri Jan 21 13:33:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Can you share the podman service logs. I think docker-compose is calling network create endpoint. Podman will/should never create a network on its own.

https://docs.docker.com/compose/networking/#use-a-pre-existing-network I think this is the solution here, you have to specify your external network as default otherwise compose will always create a default

> https://docs.docker.com/compose/networking/#use-a-pre-existing-network I think this is the solution here, you have to specify your external network as default otherwise compose will always create a default

The Docker Compose has been working with Docker and previous Podman. Is this an intended change in the new podman?
Please provide the server log, podman cannot create the network on its own docker-compose makes the api calls.
Where can I find those logs?
`journalctl -u podman.service` if you run it as systemd service otherwise just run `podman system service -t0 --log-level debug` in another terminal

Logs attached

```
# docker-compose -f /tmp/mycont.yml up -d --no-deps mycont
Creating network "tmp_default" with the default driver
Creating mycont ...
Creating mycont ... done
# podman network ls
NETWORK ID    NAME         DRIVER
39093c071800  ens160       macvlan
22e496b0a5ff  ens192       macvlan
2f259bab93aa  podman       bridge
89934527af27  tmp_default  bridge
```
[tmp_default.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7937074/tmp_default.txt)

The change in behavior is that same Docker Compose file and binary with earlier versions of Podman would not create this tmp_default network.

Could it happen that earlier podman API would return `podman` network as default one and now it has stopped doing it?

@Luap99 ,
Can we re-open the issue to see if backward compatibility can be maintained?

I still do not believe that this is a podman issue. If you look at the docker-compose output you see that it creates the network. This also matches the docker-compose documentation as linked above you have to declare your network as default.

If this work previously then this was a bug and is now fixed compose clearly makes the api call to create a network cvalled tmp_default
